q:
travel
 Nicola ___ a lot, but she doesn't go away much these days.
a: Nicola used to travel a lot, but she doesn't go away much these days.

q:
have
 Sophie ___ a motorbike, but last year she sold it and bought a car.
a: Sophie used to have a motorbike, but last year she sold it and bought a car.

q:
live
 Our friends moved to Spain a few years ago. They ___ in Paris.
a: Our friends moved to Spain a few years ago. They used to live in Paris.

q:
be
 Jackie ___ my best friend, but we aren't friends any more.
a: Jackie used to be my best friend, but we aren't friends any more.

q:
like
 I rarely eat ice cream now, but I ___ it when I was a child.
a: I rarely eat ice cream now, but I used to like it when I was a child.

q:
take
 It only takes me about 40 minutes to get to work now that the new road is open. It ___ more than an hour.
a: It only takes me about 40 minutes to get to work now that the new road is open. It used to take more than an hour.

q:
be
 There ___ a hotel near the airport, but it closed a long time ago.
a: There used to be a hotel near the airport, but it closed a long time ago.

q:
work
 I ___ in a factory. It wasn't my favourite job.
a: I used to work in a factory. It wasn't my favourite job.

q:
used to have
 Lisa ___ very long hair when she was a child.
a: Lisa used to have very long hair when she was a child.

q:
used
 We ___ to watch TV a lot, but we don't have a TV any more.
a: We used to watch TV a lot, but we don't have a TV any more.

q:
used to be
 Lisa works in a shop now. She ___ a receptionist in a hotel.
a: Lisa works in a shop now. She used to be a receptionist in a hotel.

q:
did
 What games ___ you use to play when you were a child?
a: What games did you use to play when you were a child?

q:
didn't use to
 I ___ like big cities, but now I prefer the countryside.
a: I didn't use to like big cities, but now I prefer the countryside.

q:
use
 In your last job, how many hours a day did you ___ to work?
a: In your last job, how many hours a day did you use to work?

q:
to
 I don't travel very much these days, but I used ___.
a: I don't travel very much these days, but I used to.

q:
be able
 I used to ___ to run ten kilometres, but I can't run that far now.
a: I used to be able to run ten kilometres, but I can't run that far now.

q:
didn't
 These days I eat more than before. I ___ use to eat as much.
a: These days I eat more than before. I didn't use to eat as much.

q:
play
 She ___ the piano, but she hasn't played for a long time.
a: She used to play the piano, but she hasn't played for a long time.

q:
be
 She ___ very lazy, but she works very hard these days.
a: She used to be very lazy, but she works very hard these days.

q:
not like
 She ___ cheese, but she eats lots of cheese now.
a: She didn't use to like cheese, but she eats lots of cheese now.

q:
have
 She ___ a dog, but it died two years ago.
a: She used to have a dog, but it died two years ago.

q:
never drink
 She ___ tea, but she likes it now.
a: She never used to drink tea, but she likes it now.

q:
live
 I ___ in a small village, but now I live in a city.
a: I used to live in a small village, but now I live in a city.

q:
play
 I ___ tennis a lot, but I don't play any more.
a: I used to play tennis a lot, but I don't play any more.

q:
not read
 I ___ a lot, but I do now.
a: I didn't use to read a lot, but I do now.
